You are a web designer and developer.  
Create a mobile-friendly, modern, clean personal portfolio website in HTML/CSS (you may optionally use a minimal JS for interactivity).  
Structure the website with the following pages and sections:

1. Home / Landing page  
   - Big header with my name: “Amit Kumar”  
   - Subtitle / roles beneath the name: “ Web Developer | Graphic Designer | Digital Marketer]”  
   - A tagline or short intro: “[Short Tagline about what you do or your mission]”  
   - Short “about me” paragraph summarizing who I am, my background and what I do. Use my picture as a logo of this website that I have uploaded my pic

2. Services / What I Do / Core Services section on Home or a separate Services page:  
   - List of services I offer, e.g.:  
     *Website designer and developer
     * Graphic designing - Poster, business card, logo thumbnail, and  etc
     * Digital marketing Google and Facebook ads and Instagram ads Social media management SEO email marketing Whatsapp marketing and all the digital marketing services
     * …  
   - A short description for each service.  

3. Portfolio / My Work / Projects page:  
   - A grid or list of past work/projects. For each project: name, short description, image or screenshot , link to project or more details.  https://www.gtdtrademark.com/ , https://www.grginger.com/ , https://www.cropvetcharity.org/ , https://www.prasabcpl.com/ , https://globalwarehouse.odoo.com/ , https://amproduction-creative-hub.lovable.app/ - This old link is of my previous Of website that I prepared or developed for my client across India and Australia. I have also uploded  the screenshort of my all client name With the logo. Include this also, 

4. (Optional) Blog page:  
   - Placeholder for blog posts or articles — each post with title, short excerpt, date, link to full post (you can fill real posts later).  

5. Contact / Contact Me page or section:  
   - Contact details:  
       • Email:    amitkumarrajrupdih62051@gmail.com 

       • Phone / WhatsApp  +91 6205120223 

       • Address   Bangalore, India

   - Social media or other relevant professional links https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=100094611630246 , https://www.instagram.com/growwithus__12/ , https://www.linkedin.com/in/amit-kumar62051 , Also make a Whatsapp link button with number that I have given

6. Navigation menu — across top or side — linking to Home, Portfolio, Blog (if used), Contact.  

7. Footer with copyright notice, e.g.: “© Amit Kumar | All Rights Reserved.”  

Design style guidelines:  
- Clean and modern.  
- Simple layout, easy navigation, responsive (works on mobile and desktop).  
- Light colour scheme (white/light background, clean fonts) or optionally a minimal aesthetic based on your preference.  
- Ensure that the site is easy to read and professional — suitable for a personal portfolio / freelance services showcase.  
- Use placeholder images if real project images are not yet available.  

Generate the complete HTML + CSS (and minimal JS if necessary) for the website.  
Include comments or instructions where I need to replace placeholder text or images with my real content.  
Also, output a summary of what I should replace/complete after generation (like project descriptions, real images, blog content, social links, etc.).

Okay, the structure is crystallizing. I'm prioritizing the core sections: Home, Services, Portfolio, Blog (maybe), and Contact. I'm solidifying the tech stack: React 18+, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. The user provided assets will be key, and I'll use the portfolio links to represent each item.

I'm solidifying the component breakdown: App, Home, Services, Portfolio, and Contact are the key page elements. I'm building out reusable components like Navbar, Footer, ProjectCard, and ServiceCard to keep things clean and efficient.  I'm focusing on the responsive design.

**Organizing Data & Code**

I've been thinking about the practical implementation details. I'll set up a `constants.ts` file to manage image paths for easy swapping and have now mentally structured the portfolio data as an array of objects. I'm focusing on the technical design. I'm building out a WhatsApp link generator for the contact page and choosing a vibrant Red/Rose as an accent color for the Tailwind configuration, as per client's preference. I'm also confirming the responsiveness using Tailwind classes like `md:flex` and `hidden` across various components. I've also added image paths as constants, making sure the user can easily update them with the portfolio links. I am now in the stage of building out the XML based files.


**Defining the Project Layout**

The updated `types.ts` is now ready for testing, and I'll keep an eye out for any ripple effects.


**Resolving React Import Issues**

Alright, I've come to a firm decision: adding `import React from 'react';` to `types.ts` is the most straightforward approach to fix the `React.ReactNode` type errors. While `import type { ReactNode } from 'react';` is tempting for a type-only file, the default import seems more appropriate given how `React` is generally used. It's a clean, standard solution that should resolve the namespace conflict without unnecessary complexity. I'm satisfied with this implementation.


**Importing ReactNode Directly**

I've decided to import `ReactNode` directly in `types.ts`, removing the need for a full `React` import and its associated namespace errors. I realized the existing usage is solely for `ReactNode`, which made this more aligned with best practices.
